title: Dynamic adjustment of media thumbnail image size based on touchscreen pressure
abstract: An electronic device includes a pressure-sensitive user interface component, a memory device, and a processor. The memory device has executable instructions that are executed by the processor to display a media player on a display of the electronic device. The media player includes a window for presentation of media content, a progress bar, and a play head indicating a playback position, wherein time scrolling of the media content is achieved by selection and movement of the play head along the progress bar. The method receives activation control information associated with selection of the play head, the control information including a force measurement of an amount of force imparted on the pressure-sensitive component, and the method renders a thumbnail image of the media content in response to receiving the control information, the thumbnail image having a displayed size that is influenced by the force measurement.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of controlling presentation of media content on a pressure-sensitive touchscreen display of an electronic device, the method comprising: displaying a media player on the pressure-sensitive touchscreen display, the media player comprising a primary window for presentation of media content, a progress bar, and a play head associated with the progress bar to indicate a playback position of the media content, wherein time scrolling of the media content is achieved by selection and movement of the play head along the progress bar; receiving activation control information associated with user selection of the play head during presentation of the media content, the activation control information comprising a force measurement corresponding to an amount of force imparted on or proximate to a displayed position of the play head on the pressure-sensitive touchscreen display; and rendering a thumbnail image of the media content in the media player in response to receiving the activation control information, the thumbnail image having a displayed size that is influenced by the force measurement. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the displayed size of the thumbnail image is proportional to the force measurement. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising: determining that the force measurement exceeds a threshold value; in response to the determining, maximizing the displayed size of the thumbnail image; and after maximizing the displayed size of the thumbnail image, expanding the thumbnail image into a normal playback size of the primary window. 4. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: controlling a variable granularity setting for time scrolling of the media content, in response to the force measurement. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein: higher granularity settings for time scrolling of the media content correspond to a higher number of video frames displayed during scrolling; lower granularity settings for time scrolling of the media content correspond to a lower number of video frames displayed during scrolling; and the controlling step controls the variable granularity setting to be proportional to the force measurement. 6. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: controlling a variable user feedback setting for time scrolling of the media content, in response to the force measurement. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the variable user feedback setting regulates characteristics of haptic feedback generated in association with time scrolling of the media content. 8. The method of claim 6, wherein the variable user feedback setting regulates characteristics of audio feedback generated in association with time scrolling of the media content. 9. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising: controlling a variable granularity setting for time scrolling of the media content, in response to the force measurement; and controlling a variable user feedback setting for time scrolling of the media content, in response to the force measurement. 10. An electronic device comprising: a pressure-sensitive user interface component; a memory storage device; and a processor device communicatively coupled to the memory storage device and to the pressure-sensitive user interface component, the memory storage device having computer executable instructions stored therein and configurable to be executed by the processor device to perform a method comprising: displaying a media player on a display of the electronic device, the media player comprising a primary window for presentation of media content, a progress bar, and a play head associated with the progress bar to indicate a playback position of the media content, wherein time scrolling of the media content is achieved by selection and movement of the play head along the progress bar; receiving activation control information associated with user selection of the play head during presentation of the media content, the activation control information comprising a force measurement corresponding to an amount of force imparted on the pressure-sensitive user interface component; and rendering a thumbnail image of the media content in the media player in response to receiving the activation control information, the thumbnail image having a displayed size that is influenced by the force measurement. 11. The electronic device of claim 10, wherein the displayed size of the thumbnail image is proportional to the force measurement. 12. The electronic device of claim 10 or claim 1 1, wherein the method performed by the processor device further comprises: determining that the force measurement exceeds a threshold value; in response to the determining, maximizing the displayed size of the thumbnail image; and after maximizing the displayed size of the thumbnail image, expanding the thumbnail image into a normal playback size of the primary window. 13. The electronic device of claim 10, wherein the method performed by the processor device further comprises: controlling a variable granularity setting for time scrolling of the media content, in response to the force measurement. 14. The electronic device of claim 13, wherein: higher granularity settings for time scrolling of the media content correspond to a higher number of video frames displayed during scrolling; lower granularity settings for time scrolling of the media content correspond to a lower number of video frames displayed during scrolling; and the controlling step controls the variable granularity setting to be proportional to the force measurement. 15. The electronic device of claim 10, wherein the method performed by the processor device further comprises: controlling a variable granularity setting for time scrolling of the media content, in response to the force measurement; and controlling a variable user feedback setting for time scrolling of the media content, in response to the force measurement.
